commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- Initial tasks come from props; real-time updates from other team members arrive via stream
- Call the taskUpdate action when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

stream.description: Real-time task updates from other team members

taskChanged.description: A task was moved, edited, or created by another user — update it in the board

getBoardState.description: Current board state including any unsaved edits

issues.fix: Edit button missing — only double-click editing is implemented, but requirement specifies 'editable on double-click or via edit button'

issues.description: Assignee is not editable in Add Task form — AddTaskForm captures assignee input but does not include it in taskUpdate action data payload (line 333 missing title field)
issues.fix: Assignee is not editable in Add Task form — AddTaskForm captures assignee input but does not include it in taskUpdate action data payload (line 333 missing title field)

issues.description: taskUpdate action for 'create' does not include title in data payload — only includes column and assignee (line 331-334), but title is required to complete task creation
issues.fix: taskUpdate action for 'create' does not include title in data payload — only includes column and assignee (line 331-334), but title is required to complete task creation

issues.fix: Add Task button (line 440-447) has no aria-label describing its purpose or target column

issues.description: Dropdown onChange handler (line 180) does not receive a string value parameter — ensure dropdown correctly passes selected value as string to onMove callback
issues.fix: Dropdown onChange handler (line 180) does not receive a string value parameter — ensure dropdown correctly passes selected value as string to onMove callback

issues.description: Column container (line 381) uses aria-label with dynamic content but could benefit from explicit heading role for better screen reader navigation
issues.fix: Column container (line 381) uses aria-label with dynamic content but could benefit from explicit heading role for better screen reader navigation

issues.description: Progress component (line 359-365) has showLabel prop but no aria-label for the progress indicator itself
issues.fix: Progress component (line 359-365) has showLabel prop but no aria-label for the progress indicator itself

evaluation.critique: The component is incomplete (truncated at Card closing tag) and has several critical issues: missing the main board layout with columns, incomplete task card implementation, and inline style overrides that conflict with design tokens (backgroundColor in onMouseEnter/Leave). While some design tokens are used correctly (var(--ggui-spacing-*)), many hardcoded values and inline styles undermine consistency.
